name: New Era Energy & Digital, Inc.

profile.description: New Era Energy & Digital operates as a developer and operator of next-generation digital infrastructure and integrated power assets for AI hyperscalers. Its flagship project is Texas Critical Data Centers (TCDC) in Ector County, Texas, covering 438 square kilometers. The company was formerly New Era Helium and changed its name in August 2025. Headquartered in Midland, Texas.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): The company pivoted from helium extraction to AI data center infrastructure in August 2025, raising $115M in equity and securing a $20M Macquarie credit facility with potential for $270M more, but has delivered zero revenue from the new business line — FY2025 revenue of $885K is entirely legacy helium operations [1][2]. AI Deployment (0/2): NUAI builds physical infrastructure intended for AI hyperscaler tenants but deploys no AI technology itself; the company is a landlord-in-development, not a technology operator. Market Share (0/2): The company has zero operational data center capacity and no market share in digital infrastructure, competing against established operators like Equinix, Digital Realty, and QTS with decades of track record [5]. Pipeline (1/2): The TCDC campus has a 1+ GW long-term vision with a non-binding LOI from Stream Data Centers and Macquarie financing secured, but no construction milestones, power interconnection agreements, or binding customer commitments have been disclosed [2]. R&D Intensity (0/1): Spending is capex-oriented land acquisition ($71.7M TCDC asset) rather than R&D; the company has no technology development program [1].

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): The stated purpose of building 'next-generation digital infrastructure and integrated power assets for AI hyperscalers' is generic and interchangeable with dozens of data center startups competing for the same hyperscaler contracts [1]. There is no articulated vision of societal benefit beyond capitalizing on AI demand. Community Impact (0/2): With only 5 full-time employees and no disclosed charitable programs, community engagement, or social investment initiatives, there is no meaningful community footprint to evaluate [1]. Employee Retention (0/2): The company has 5 employees, making organizational culture metrics meaningless; the recent hires of a COO, CDO, GC, and CCO in early 2026 suggest the team is still being assembled [1]. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO E. Will Gray II holds approximately 17.5% of shares outstanding, demonstrating significant personal financial exposure to the company's success, though this is predominantly founder's equity rather than recent open-market purchases [5].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: NUAI's financial profile is characteristic of a pre-revenue development-stage company with extreme leverage and no profitability. D/E of 520% reflects $52.5M in debt against negative equity of -$2.6M, far exceeding the 70% target [5]. ROE of -9.59% and profit margin of 0.0% confirm the company generates no earnings — FY2025 net loss was $29.6M on just $885K revenue, widening 115% year-over-year [1]. Revenue growth of 145.8% YoY is misleading, representing an increase from $533K to $885K in legacy helium sales, not data center revenue. Free cash flow is -$1.7M with operating cash flow of -$15.3M, and total cash of $2.2M against $52.5M debt creates significant liquidity risk despite the Macquarie facility [1][5]. The sole positive metric is daily dollar volume of approximately $43.2M, easily clearing the $1M liquidity floor [5].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (5/9): NUAI's entire business thesis is building physical infrastructure to serve AI hyperscaler demand, meaning AI growth directly benefits the company's addressable market — the 438 sq km TCDC site is positioned specifically for large-scale AI compute campuses [1][2]. However, the company has no operational moat, no contracted customers, and faces intense competition from established data center operators (Equinix, Digital Realty, CyrusOne) and well-capitalized new entrants backed by hyperscalers building their own facilities. The land position in West Texas provides access to cheap power but is not a defensible competitive advantage — multiple competitors are pursuing similar strategies in Texas and other power-rich regions. AI Opportunity (1/1): The company's entire revenue model is predicated on AI infrastructure demand; if the TCDC project reaches operational status, 100% of data center revenue would be AI-driven, and the Stream Data Centers LOI specifically targets AI workload hosting [2].

infinite_game.summary: New Era Energy & Digital exhibits a finite mindset driven by financial opportunism rather than enduring purpose. The pivot from helium extraction to AI data centers was motivated by market opportunity rather than a Just Cause, and the securities fraud allegations covering November 2024 through December 2025 raise serious questions about leadership integrity [3]. With only 5 employees, no organizational culture to evaluate, and a business model built entirely around capitalizing on the AI infrastructure boom, the company lacks every hallmark of Sinek's infinite game framework.

just_cause.analysis: The company articulates no Just Cause beyond capturing AI data center demand. 'Building next-generation digital infrastructure for AI hyperscalers' is a market opportunity description, not an enduring purpose that would survive a strategic pivot or market downturn [1]. The rapid name change from New Era Helium to New Era Energy & Digital in August 2025 reinforces the perception that the company follows capital flows rather than a durable mission. There is no evidence of commitment to a cause that transcends profit maximization. (0/3 points, floor applied)

trusting_teams.analysis: With only 5 full-time employees and a leadership team assembled in early 2026 (COO Charlie Nelson hired January 2026, CDO, GC, and CCO added shortly after), there is no organizational depth to evaluate for psychological safety or trust culture [1]. The securities fraud lawsuit filed April 2026 alleging a scheme involving transferring wells among related entities and placing liability-bearing companies into bankruptcy to avoid environmental obligations directly undermines trust in leadership integrity [3]. (0/2 points)

worthy_rivals.analysis: There is no evidence of an infinite competitive mindset. The company does not acknowledge or learn from established data center operators like Equinix or Digital Realty; instead, it positions its TCDC project as a differentiated play based on land scale and power access without recognizing the operational expertise gap between a 5-person startup and multi-billion-dollar incumbents [2]. (0/1 point)

existential_flexibility.analysis: The pivot from helium extraction to AI data center infrastructure represents genuine strategic transformation — the company abandoned its legacy natural gas and helium business entirely to pursue a fundamentally different market opportunity in 2025 [1]. The acquisition of TCDC's 438 sq km site and $115M equity raise demonstrate committed capital reallocation. However, this flexibility appears driven by financial desperation and market opportunity rather than service to a Just Cause, and the environmental liability allegations suggest the legacy business was abandoned without responsible stewardship [3][4]. (1/2 points)

courage_to_lead.analysis: The securities fraud complaint filed April 2026 alleges a fraudulent scheme involving transferring wells among related entities and placing liability-bearing companies into bankruptcy to avoid plugging and remediation costs — the antithesis of courageous leadership in Sinek's framework [3]. While the New Mexico environmental lawsuit was settled with full dismissal and no admission of liability in June 2026, the pattern of allegations suggests leadership that prioritizes financial engineering over principled stewardship of obligations [4]. Courage to Lead requires acting in service of a Just Cause even when costly; these allegations describe the opposite. (0/2 points)

investment_thesis: New Era Energy & Digital is a high-risk, early-stage bet on AI infrastructure demand, offering potential asymmetric upside if the TCDC project in Ector County, Texas, reaches operational scale. The 438 sq km site provides one of the largest contiguous land positions available for data center development, and the company has secured meaningful financing — $115M in equity, a $20M Macquarie credit facility with potential expansion to $270M, and a non-binding LOI with Stream Data Centers [1][2]. The AI infrastructure buildout is a secular megatrend with hyperscalers collectively planning hundreds of billions in data center capex through 2030, and NUAI's West Texas location offers access to abundant, low-cost power — a critical competitive factor as AI compute power consumption scales exponentially. CEO Gray's 17.5% ownership stake aligns management incentives with shareholders, and the recent leadership hires (COO, CDO, GC, CCO) suggest the company is building organizational capacity to execute [5].
thesis_continued: The bear case is overwhelming at this stage. NUAI is a pre-revenue development company with $885K in legacy helium revenue, a $29.6M net loss, negative equity of -$2.6M, and 520% debt-to-equity — the financial profile of a company that may not survive long enough to deliver on its vision [1][5]. The securities fraud lawsuit filed April 2026 alleges a fraudulent scheme involving related-party asset transfers and bankruptcy abuse to avoid environmental liabilities, creating existential legal and reputational risk [3]. The company has only 5 employees, no operational data center capacity, no binding customer contracts, and no proven ability to construct or operate data center infrastructure at any scale. Competition is intense — Equinix, Digital Realty, QTS, and hyperscaler-backed new entrants all have multi-year head starts, established customer relationships, and operational expertise that NUAI cannot match. The stock's 12.6% short interest and 52-week range of $0.32-$9.45 reflect extreme speculative volatility [5]. With a $599M market cap built entirely on optionality and zero demonstrated execution capability, the risk-reward is heavily skewed to the downside.
key_catalysts: Conversion of the Stream Data Centers LOI into a binding lease or build-to-suit agreement would validate the TCDC site's commercial viability and provide the first contracted revenue visibility [2]
Drawdown of the Macquarie credit facility's potential $270M expansion and commencement of Phase 1 construction at TCDC would demonstrate the project's progression from concept to execution [2]
Favorable resolution of the April 2026 securities fraud lawsuit would remove the most significant legal overhang suppressing the company's valuation and institutional investor interest [3]
key_risks: Securities fraud lawsuit covering November 2024 through December 2025 alleges a fraudulent scheme with related-party transfers and bankruptcy abuse, creating existential legal liability and reputational damage that could deter partners and investors [3]

Extreme financial fragility with negative equity (-$2.6M), $52.5M debt, $2.2M cash, and -$15.3M operating cash flow means the company is dependent on continued access to capital markets to survive — any financing disruption is fatal [1][5]

Zero operational data center capacity and no binding customer contracts against entrenched competition from Equinix, Digital Realty, and hyperscaler self-builds means execution risk is near-total on the core business thesis [2][5]
sources: New Era Energy & Digital Q1 2026 10-Q Filing — net loss $8.99M, TCDC acquisition ($71.7M land), pivot to AI digital infrastructure, total assets $86.5M, SEC filing 2026
New Era Energy & Digital Financing — $115M equity offering, $20M Macquarie credit facility ($270M potential expansion), non-binding LOI with Stream Data Centers, company press releases 2025-2026

Securities Fraud Complaint — filed April 1, 2026, covering November 2024 through December 2025, alleging fraudulent scheme with legacy helium assets, related-party transfers, and misleading disclosures
New Mexico Environmental Lawsuit — legacy helium/gas environmental obligation claims, settled with full dismissal and no admission of liability, June 2026
Yahoo Finance / StockAnalysis — NUAI key statistics, $5.92 stock price, $599.6M market cap, 7.3M avg daily volume, 520.25% D/E, 12.61% short interest, accessed June 2026
